content:
Its Free and open source so give it a try..

7 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

SourceI want to say upfront that I’m not here to tell you to stop vibe coding. I do it every day. I’m not stopping.
But something happened over the last few months that I’ve been chewing on.
I stopped reading the code.
Not on purpose. It just kind of happened. The AI writes 200 lines in 8 seconds, the tests pass, the thing works in the browser, and there’s another ticket sitting there. Somewhere in that loop “does it work” quietly replaced “is this okay” and nobody sent me a memo about it.
So last week I actually went and looked at what we’re all shipping.
The number that got me
Veracode tested over 100 AI models. 45% of the code they generated had a real security hole in it — not “could be cleaner,” an actual OWASP Top 10 vulnerability.
Fine. Bad, but fine. Here’s the part I can’t shake:
The bigger, newer models weren’t better.
This isn’t a gap that closes with the next release. Writing code that runs and writing code that survives someone actively trying to break it are two different jobs, and only one of them is being optimised for. We’ve…
